ignorePatterns.comment: External links only. markdown-link-check has a reproducible bug resolving parent-relative local links (e.g. '../adr/ADR-0005.md' from a nested file reports dead even when the target exists) - confirmed independent of any config, in complete isolation, across tool versions 3.11-3.14. Internal cross-references also include known-absent code paths (src/, scripts/, tests/, packages/, corpus/) already tracked as their own staged debt in docs/governance/Migration-Plan.md. Scoping to https?:// sidesteps the bug and avoids re-flagging already-catalogued debt, at the cost of not catching internal cross-reference rot - a tradeoff, not a gap discovered later.
Social platforms that reliably 403 automated / non-browser clients regardless of User-Agent - not a signal about link health.
Local dev-server instructions in docs (e.g. dbt/crystalcore_emotion_warehouse/README.md's 'dbt docs serve' step) - never a real endpoint to check.

crystalarchitect.dev genuinely fails DNS resolution (confirmed via getent, not a sandbox/proxy artifact) - but its only two references (COMMERCIAL_LICENSE.md, LICENSING.md) are already-superseded historical docs per ADR-0010, kept for provenance. Not this check's job to guess a replacement domain for frozen content.

PyPI's account-tokens page, referenced only from the three superseded packages/-era publishing guides (docs/PUBLISHING.md, docs/FIRST_RELEASE.md, docs/RESTRUCTURING_COMPLETE.md) - moot until a real PyPI publish is attempted.
